commands: web search: MeV GeV secluded dark sector BBN CMB constraints dark radiation Delta Neff
web search: vector dark matter self interaction constraints review dwarf cluster cross section mass MeV GeV
web search: Higgs portal scalar singlet constraints invisible direct detection review
web search: classically conformal dark sector NANOGrav BBN CMB energy injection
web search: Dark Sectors 2016 Workshop community report beam dump constraints portal assumptions arXiv
web search: US Cosmic Visions New Ideas in Dark Matter 2017 dark sectors portal constraints arXiv
warnings: No exclusion is claimed because the required cosmological, portal, abundance, and self-interaction observables are mostly absent.
Portal-based laboratory constraints are not applicable to the minimal zero-portal model.
PTArcade MAP has extremely strong supercooling; template and thermal-history caveats are important.
One decoupled-hidden-sector item is metadata/search-context only and is marked citation_verified=false.
approximations: m_chi range estimated from m_chi = sqrt(9*(gD/2)^4/(8*pi^2))*vD using the model handoff expression for beta_lambda.
Derived mass range uses independent extrema of the 1-sigma marginal intervals.

notes: Minimal model has portal_coupling=0, no Abelian kinetic mixing, and no dark fermions.

constraints.name: BBN/CMB bounds on MeV-scale thermal dark sectors
constraints.description: Thermally coupled MeV-scale BSM states can alter expansion history, light-element abundances, and CMB observables.
constraints.observable: light thermal relic impact on expansion and light-element/CMB observables
constraints.applicability: Overlaps in mass scale but not directly in assumptions; published thermal-SM bounds require thermal coupling assumptions absent in the minimal secluded handoffs.
applicability_notes: Do not apply from mass alone.

source.title: Refined Bounds on MeV-scale Thermal Dark Sectors from BBN and the CMB
source.authors: Nashwan Sabti, James Alvey, Miguel Escudero, Malcolm Fairbairn, Diego Blas

constraints.name: Updated BBN/CMB MeV dark-sector bounds after LUNA rate
constraints.description: Update/robustness check for the MeV-scale thermal dark-sector BBN/CMB bounds.
constraints.applicability: Useful update source; no direct application without thermal history.
applicability_notes: Not an independent exclusion relative to C01.

source.title: Addendum: Refined bounds on MeV-scale thermal dark sectors from BBN and the CMB
source.authors: Nashwan Sabti, James Alvey, Miguel Escudero, Malcolm Fairbairn, Diego Blas

constraints.name: Delta N_eff / dark radiation from secluded sector
constraints.description: Extra relativistic energy density at BBN or recombination is constrained by CMB/BBN measurements.

conventions.Planck result: N_eff parameter in extended LCDM fits

constraints.applicability: Relevant if the dark sector was populated or has residual radiation; not testable from masses alone.
applicability_notes: Planck gives N_eff constraints, but model mapping is missing.

constraints.name: BBN/CMB energy release from PTA-scale dark phase transition
constraints.description: A strongly supercooled dark FOPT near sub-GeV temperatures can affect cosmology depending on reheating and energy partition.
constraints.applicability: Important caveat for strongly supercooled MAP; no direct exclusion from GW fit alone.
applicability_notes: Benchmark context only; primary region is 1sigma posterior.

source.title: Does NANOGrav observe a dark sector phase transition?
source.authors: Torsten Bringmann, Paul Frederik Depta, Thomas Konstandin, Kai Schmidt-Hoberg, Carlo Tasillo

constraints.name: Self-interacting dark matter limits from halo/cluster structure
constraints.description: Astrophysical structure constrains DM self-interactions if the dark vectors constitute a stable DM component.
constraints.observable: sigma_T/m as a function of velocity

conventions.self-interaction: transfer or viscosity cross section must match source convention

constraints.applicability: Relevant only under a DM-stability/abundance assumption.

tested_subregion.mass_overlap: full 1sigma vector mass range; no exclusion

applicability_notes: Do not compare mass/gD alone to SIDM bounds.

source.title: Dark matter self-interactions and small scale structure

constraints.name: Bullet Cluster / merging-cluster self-interaction upper bounds
constraints.description: Merging clusters constrain cluster-scale self-interaction cross section per unit mass for dominant DM.
constraints.applicability: Requires computed cross section and the assumption that W_D is a relevant DM component.

tested_subregion.mass_overlap: full 1sigma vector mass range; no exclusion

applicability_notes: Useful as a target observable, not a direct bound here.

source.title: Direct constraints on the dark matter self-interaction cross-section from the merging galaxy cluster 1E0657-56

constraints.name: Higgs-portal invisible/exotic Higgs decay constraints
constraints.description: LHC Higgs invisible/exotic decays constrain nonzero Higgs-portal couplings to light hidden sectors.
constraints.observable: Higgs invisible or exotic branching fraction
constraints.applicability: Not applicable in the minimal zero-portal model.
applicability_notes: Would become relevant in non-minimal portal extensions.

source.title: The Higgs-portal for dark matter: effective field theories versus concrete realizations

constraints.name: Higgs-portal direct detection constraints
constraints.description: Direct detection constrains DM-nucleon scattering mediated by a nonzero portal.
constraints.applicability: Not applicable with zero portal and no relic-abundance assumption.

source.title: The Higgs-portal for dark matter: effective field theories versus concrete realizations

constraints.name: Beam-dump/fixed-target dark-sector searches
constraints.description: Sub-GeV dark-sector searches in beam dumps require portal-mediated production and visible/invisible detector signatures.

coupling_range.kinetic_mixing: absent for non-Abelian SU(2)_D before breaking

constraints.observable: production and decay/scattering rates in fixed-target experiments
constraints.applicability: Not applicable to the secluded minimal model.
applicability_notes: Mass overlap with beam-dump ranges is not enough.

constraints.name: Collider and accelerator portal searches for light hidden sectors
constraints.description: Community-report light-dark-sector search program assumes explicit portal interactions to produce/detect hidden states.
constraints.observable: portal-mediated production, missing energy, displaced or visible decays
constraints.applicability: Not applicable without an SM portal.
applicability_notes: Do not apply collider limits from mass alone.

source.title: US Cosmic Visions: New Ideas in Dark Matter 2017: Community Report

constraints.name: Thermalization / decoupling of secluded dark sector
constraints.description: A secluded sector can still face cosmological constraints depending on initial temperature and whether it thermalized with the SM.
constraints.applicability: Central missing calculation for interpreting cosmological bounds.
applicability_notes: Citation was not fetched beyond metadata/search result; no quantitative limit inferred.

source.title: Observational Constraints on Decoupled Hidden Sectors

constraints.name: Perturbativity and unitarity of dark SU(2) coupling
constraints.description: The posterior gauge coupling can be checked against the perturbative domain used by the upstream model validation.
constraints.observable: coupling size and CW expansion validity

conventions.criterion: gD < sqrt(4*pi) from upstream FOPT validation domain

constraints.applicability: Directly testable and passed as a basic consistency check; no exclusion applied.
applicability_notes: Higher-loop/RG and strong-supercooling validity remain separate caveats.

missing_calculations.observable: dark-sector temperature ratio T_dark/T_SM and decoupling history

validation_checks: compare to benchmark thermal relic limits

missing_calculations.observable: vector self-interaction transfer cross section sigma_T/m(v)
dark-vector relic abundance or fractional DM abundance
portal-induced widths, lifetimes, and branching fractions
higher-order/RG validity of strongly supercooled CW FOPT
